slices.title: Push next-best actions, keyboard convenience, and bulk-flow safety deeper into mission, triage, story, and route handling

slices.exit_condition: The highest-frequency mission-to-triage-to-story-to-route flows require fewer context switches, fewer scroll jumps, and less operator recall while preserving reversible mutations and clear danger semantics.
notes: Favor card-local quick actions and next-step affordances over adding more global controls.
Keep bulk operations safe and obvious; faster should not mean easier to misfire.

slices.title: Productize onboarding and empty states so the browser teaches the lifecycle instead of assuming prior CLI context
slices.exit_condition: A first-time operator can understand the browser lifecycle path from mission creation to triage, story promotion, and route setup without needing to start in CLI documentation.
notes: Guidance should be contextual and brief instead of turning the shell into a documentation wall.
This slice is about reducing onboarding friction, not adding a generic walkthrough library.

slices.title: Harden browser-level regression coverage for deep links, saved views, dock behavior, triage-to-story flow, and route CRUD
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_triage.py tests/test_watchlist.py tests/test_alerts.py tests/test_story.py -q
slices.exit_condition: The most stateful browser flows have repeatable regression coverage strong enough to protect further console productization and layout work from silent breakage.
notes: Prefer high-signal operating-flow checks over brittle visual snapshots.
If UI churn accelerates, this slice can be pulled earlier even though it is listed last in the preferred ignition order.

phases.title: Console Operating-Surface Refactor Blueprint
phases.goal: Turn the lifecycle-complete browser shell into a lower-noise, state-driven workbench with one coherent navigation model, clearer cross-stage continuity, explicit card-action hierarchy, and predictable desktop/mobile behavior.

slices.title: Land a repo-scoped GUI refactor blueprint covering information architecture, navigation convergence, card action priority, and desktop/mobile interaction rules
slices.exit_condition: The repository contains an explicit GUI refactor blueprint and a machine-readable slice map that can be used as the next manual ignition feedstock.
notes: This slice promotes the UX judgment into repo-truth artifacts instead of keeping it as chat-only design context.
The blueprint must stay aligned to the Reader-backed lifecycle contract and must not introduce GUI-only nouns.

slices.title: Converge console navigation into one primary rail plus one secondary context layer without overlapping meta-navigation systems
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_watchlist.py tests/test_triage.py tests/test_story.py -q
slices.exit_condition: Operators can move across intake, missions, review, and delivery through one primary navigation model while command palette, deep links, and saved views remain accelerators rather than competing top-level entry systems.
notes: Collapse primary wayfinding before adding more card-level convenience affordances.
Preserve deep-link and saved-view semantics, but demote them from co-primary nav to contextual acceleration.

slices.title: Recompose mission, review, and delivery information architecture around lifecycle continuity instead of copy-only explanation
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_watchlist.py tests/test_triage.py tests/test_story.py tests/test_alerts.py -q
slices.exit_condition: Mission, triage, story, and delivery workspaces expose visible cross-stage continuity facts and tighter list-detail-editor relationships, reducing context switching across the browser shell.
notes: This slice should surface lifecycle continuity in the working surface rather than expanding onboarding prose.
Cross-stage counters and next-hop facts should be additive views over existing Reader-backed objects.

slices.title: Apply a state-driven card action hierarchy across mission, triage, story, and route surfaces
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_watchlist.py tests/test_triage.py tests/test_story.py tests/test_alerts.py -q
slices.exit_condition: High-frequency cards expose one obvious primary CTA, calmer secondary action density, and clearer danger-action treatment that matches card state instead of static button rows.
notes: Primary CTA selection should be state-driven rather than card-type-only.
Destructive actions should move behind clearer danger treatments or overflow rather than staying in every quick-action row.

slices.title: Normalize desktop and mobile interaction behavior around one responsive density and pane contract
slices.exit_condition: Desktop and mobile console behavior follows one explicit pane, density, modal, and action-sheet contract instead of ad hoc breakpoint-specific layout exceptions.
notes: Small-screen behavior should favor one dominant pane and one dominant action at a time.
Brand language should survive breakpoint changes without letting decorative chrome outrank operational facts.

slices.title: Harden verification for navigation convergence, card-priority behavior, and responsive interaction regressions
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_watchlist.py tests/test_triage.py tests/test_story.py tests/test_alerts.py -q
slices.exit_condition: The console refactor wave has repeatable workflow-level regression coverage for primary navigation, context restore, state-driven CTA behavior, and responsive interaction safety.
notes: Prefer high-signal operating-flow checks over brittle visual snapshots.
This slice should lock in the new operating model before another round of browser-surface expansion.

phases.title: Console Workbench Convergence And Live Desk Refactor
phases.goal: Turn the refactored operating surface into an object-first analyst desk where populated workspaces lead with current-object facts, desktop uses stable workbench panes, and lifecycle handoff remains visible across Mission -> Review -> Story -> Delivery.

slices.title: Promote the next workbench-convergence blueprint and manual ignition map into repository truth
slices.exit_condition: The repository contains an explicit L13 workbench-convergence blueprint and the active blueprint path exposes a next manual ignition target again.
notes: This slice promotes the latest UI/UXD workbench judgment into repo-truth artifacts instead of leaving it as chat-only critique.
The promoted follow-up map intentionally stays inside the Reader-backed browser shell rather than opening a premature frontend-stack rewrite track.

slices.title: Refactor intake into a live desk that leads with current-object facts, pressure signals, and next actions when data exists
slices.exit_condition: A populated browser shell opens on current-object facts and next-step actions before decorative hero treatment or copy-heavy guidance.
notes: Mission drafting, presets, and clone support should remain accessible, but they should no longer dominate the first live operating view.
Empty or first-run states may keep richer onboarding treatment; populated states should not.

slices.title: Promote the topbar context into a persistent object rail that preserves mission, evidence, story, and route handoff across stages
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_watchlist.py tests/test_triage.py tests/test_story.py -q
slices.exit_condition: Moving from mission to evidence to story to route no longer feels like opening disconnected desks; one visible handoff chain remains in view.
notes: Saved views, deep links, and command palette remain accelerators rather than becoming the primary object-memory system.
Reversible mutations should return operators to the same object context unless the mutation explicitly promotes the work to a new stage.

slices.title: Normalize desktop workspaces into stable list-detail-support panes for mission, review, story, and delivery handling
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_watchlist.py tests/test_triage.py tests/test_story.py tests/test_alerts.py -q
slices.exit_condition: Desktop work no longer depends on repeated full-page scroll traversal for mission, triage, story, and delivery handling.
notes: Selection-heavy flows should prefer stable pane relationships over stacked report-style page sections.
Preserve list scroll position and detail focus after state mutations where possible.

slices.title: Tighten the review-story evidence lane and split story workspace into board and editor modes
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_triage.py tests/test_story.py -q
slices.exit_condition: Operators can promote evidence into narrative work without reselecting context or parsing one long story report surface.
notes: Treat triage-to-story movement as one evidence lane rather than two loosely adjacent pages.
Story board and story editor modes should be explicit enough to reduce long-form detail sprawl.

slices.title: Compress populated-workspace control density so chips summarize state and one dominant action stays legible
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_triage.py tests/test_story.py tests/test_alerts.py -q
slices.exit_condition: Populated cards and workspaces scan faster, with less instructional reading and less control-priority flattening.
notes: Chips should summarize state more often than they create peer-level button walls.
Instructional prose should collapse once the relevant workspace already contains real operational data.

slices.title: Harden verification for live-desk behavior, object-rail persistence, pane stability, and mobile one-task posture
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_watchlist.py tests/test_triage.py tests/test_story.py tests/test_alerts.py -q
slices.exit_condition: The next workbench refactor wave has regression coverage for the new object-first operating model before another round of shell expansion lands.
notes: Add browser-level assertions for populated-intake behavior, current-object rail persistence, pane-mode stability, and story editor transitions.
Protect mobile one-dominant-pane behavior before further populated-state refactors land.

phases.title: Research OS Report Production Blueprint And Follow-up
phases.goal: Extend the lifecycle-complete intelligence console with a structured report-production layer above Story so DataPulse can assemble, gate, and export reusable research assets instead of stopping at story export.

slices.title: Promote the repo-scoped Research OS report-production blueprint and manual ignition map into repository truth
slices.exit_condition: The repository contains a repo-scoped report-production blueprint and the active blueprint path exposes a new next manual ignition target beyond the completed console workbench wave.
notes: This slice promotes the latest Research OS judgment into repo truth instead of leaving it as chat-only or Obsidian-only context.
The promoted map must stay additive to the Reader-backed lifecycle rather than reopening the console as a second product.

slices.title: Extend the lifecycle contract and roadmap with report-layer nouns, stage mapping, and invariants
slices.exit_condition: The repo has one explicit contract of record for how Story, report-layer objects, and report export relate, without treating report work as ad hoc story export.
notes: Preserve Story as the evidence package layer and extend the lifecycle above it rather than redefining existing nouns.
This slice should define invariants and stage mapping before core models or browser work lands.

slices.title: Add report-layer core objects and persistence without introducing GUI-only state
slices.exit_condition: Report objects can be created, loaded, updated, and listed through repo-core persistence without being hidden inside Story strings or browser-local state.
notes: Start with ReportBrief, ClaimCard, ReportSection, CitationBundle, Report, and ExportProfile as first-class repo objects.
Keep persistence additive to current story storage instead of coupling reports into UI drafts.

slices.title: Add claim composition, citation bundles, and report quality guardrails in core
slices.exit_condition: Report assembly can evaluate claim-source binding, section coverage, contradictions, and export gates in core logic before any browser composition surface depends on it.
notes: Treat uncited claims, single-source sections, freshness gaps, and unresolved contradictions as operator-visible guardrail outputs.
Do not hide quality or provenance checks inside one opaque export renderer.

slices.title: Expose report CRUD, compose, quality, and export through Reader, CLI, MCP, and HTTP API surfaces
verification_commands: python3 -m py_compile datapulse/reader.py datapulse/cli.py datapulse/mcp_server.py datapulse/console_server.py
slices.exit_condition: Report-layer nouns are available through the same Reader-backed surfaces used by CLI, MCP, and browser flows rather than being trapped behind one UI path.
notes: Add report CRUD plus compose, quality-preview, and export endpoints without forking naming or status semantics by surface.
Keep browser work dependent on the same Reader-backed report APIs.

slices.title: Add browser Claim Composer and Report Studio workspaces inside the current console shell
slices.exit_condition: Operators can compose claims into report sections and inspect quality guardrails in the browser without introducing a second frontend product or browser-only report state.
notes: Stay inside the current console shell and current Reader-backed contract.
The browser should project persisted claim and report objects rather than simulating them locally.

slices.title: Add brief, full, sources, and watch-pack export profiles plus report-to-watch feedback hooks
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_report.py tests/test_watchlist.py tests/test_console_server.py -q
slices.exit_condition: One report object can emit reusable brief, full, source-pack, and watch-pack outputs, and the watch-pack path can feed follow-up mission refinement without manual copy-paste.
notes: Treat export profiles as reusable report assets rather than alternate skins over one Markdown blob.
The watch-pack path should stay additive to current mission creation semantics.

slices.title: Harden verification for report persistence, claim traceability, quality gates, and end-to-end export flows
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_report.py tests/test_story.py tests/test_watchlist.py -q
slices.exit_condition: The new report layer has regression coverage for persistence, claim-source traceability, guardrail blocking, export profiles, and browser composition flow before another expansion wave lands.
notes: Prefer high-signal end-to-end assertions over brittle snapshots.
Protect claim traceability and export safety before tuning richer editorial affordances.

phases.title: Report-Backed Delivery And Subscription Follow-up
phases.goal: Extend authoritative report outputs into one Reader-backed delivery and subscription plane so DataPulse can subscribe to, route, and audit report-derived packages without inventing GUI-only delivery state or a second lifecycle.

slices.title: Promote the repo-scoped report-delivery and subscription blueprint plus ignition map into repository truth
slices.exit_condition: The repository contains a repo-scoped blueprint for report-backed delivery and normalized subscriptions, and the active blueprint path exposes a new manual ignition target beyond the completed report-production wave.
notes: Promote the next-wave route map into repo truth before reopening implementation slices.
Keep this slice limited to blueprint and roadmap truth rather than contract or code work.

slices.title: Extend lifecycle and delivery contracts with report-authoritative output kinds and normalized subscription invariants
slices.exit_condition: The repo has one explicit contract of record for how report outputs become authoritative delivery subjects and how normalized subscriptions relate to routes, output kinds, and delivery observations.
notes: Extend the current delivery contract rather than bypassing it with report-specific ad hoc terms.
Treat report-backed delivery as additive to alerts, feeds, and story export.

slices.title: Add normalized delivery-subscription core objects and persistence without forking route truth
slices.exit_condition: The repo can persist normalized delivery-subscription state for report, story, watch, and profile subjects using explicit subject, output, route, and cursor semantics instead of CLI-only flags or browser-local toggles.
notes: Keep named routes as reusable sink identities rather than duplicating transport secrets inline.
Do not copy report or story business fields into subscription records.

slices.title: Add report-backed package assembly and attributable dispatch records above current alert events
slices.exit_condition: Report-backed pull and push packages can be assembled deterministically, and route-backed dispatch produces attributable event or attempt records before transport.
notes: Preserve provenance back to report and export-profile truth.
Treat dispatch records as delivery observations, not as replacements for report truth.

slices.title: Expose delivery-subscription CRUD and report-backed dispatch through Reader, CLI, MCP, and HTTP API surfaces
verification_commands: python3 -m py_compile datapulse/reader.py datapulse/cli.py datapulse/mcp_server.py datapulse/console_server.py
slices.exit_condition: Normalized delivery-subscription nouns and report-backed dispatch are available through the same Reader-backed surfaces used by CLI, MCP, and browser flows.
notes: Keep naming and status semantics aligned across every surface.
Browser work should depend on the same Reader-backed delivery APIs.

slices.title: Add a browser delivery workspace for subscriptions, route-backed dispatch, and report package audit inside the current console shell
slices.exit_condition: Operators can subscribe to, inspect, and dispatch report-backed output packages in the browser without introducing a second frontend product or browser-only delivery state.
notes: Stay inside the current shell and current Reader-backed contract.
The browser should project persisted delivery objects rather than simulate them locally.

slices.title: Harden verification for route-backed report delivery, subscription normalization, and delivery audit flows
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_delivery.py tests/test_console_server.py tests/test_report.py -q
slices.exit_condition: The report-backed delivery plane has regression coverage for subscription persistence, route-backed dispatch, audit records, and browser workflow before richer briefing output work lands.
notes: Prefer high-signal end-to-end assertions over brittle snapshots.
Protect delivery auditability and route semantics before adding richer briefing affordances.

notes: The exporter should bind existing evidence rather than inventing a second readiness summary.
Bundle, runtime-hit, sidecar, and replay entrypoint must be attributable to the same declared window.

slices.title: Harden promotion gating and verification around attestation freshness and same-window consistency
verification_commands: uv run python -m pytest tests/test_delivery.py tests/test_governance_loop.py -q
slices.exit_condition: Promotion and replay gates read the release-window attestation as the primary same-window truth and reject stale, missing, or cross-head evidence while still accepting report_draft verified_fail_closed as required runtime closure.
notes: Prefer same-window consistency checks over adding new semantic readiness knobs.
The attestation should become the first file inspected during manual promotion review.

phases.title: QNAIGC search provider as a cost-aware Chinese-query candidate lane
phases.goal: Add a narrow QNAIGC search-provider lane through SearchGateway without changing SearchHit normalization, default explicit providers, or operator-visible cost control.

slices.title: Land the repo-scoped QNAIGC search-provider blueprint and manual ignition map
slices.exit_condition: The repository contains a repo-scoped blueprint that defines QNAIGC as a cost-aware Chinese-query candidate provider, and the active blueprint truth exposes a new manual ignition target beyond the completed L17 same-window attestation wave.
fact_sources: https://developer.qiniu.com/aitokenapi/13192/web-search-api
notes: Treat operator-provided cost guidance as an explicit planning constraint rather than a hidden implementation assumption.
This slice should promote repo truth for routing, provenance, and ignition order without prematurely claiming runtime support.

slices.title: Define the QNAIGC provider contract routing rules and runtime config boundary
slices.exit_condition: Repo truth explicitly defines how QNAIGC token lookup, Chinese-query candidate routing, provider precedence, provider-specific extra fields, and cost-aware activation constraints should work before adapter code lands.
notes: Keep authority_score, date, and request_id in SearchHit.extra rather than promoting them to first-class cross-provider fields.
The contract should preserve current explicit jina and tavily modes while allowing qnaigc as a narrow auto candidate.

slices.title: Add the QNAIGC provider adapter normalized SearchHit mapping and Chinese-query auto candidate routing
slices.exit_condition: SearchGateway can call QNAIGC behind runtime secrets, preserve SearchHit normalization, and only introduce it as an auto candidate for Chinese-language queries without replacing current explicit-provider behavior.
notes: Reuse the existing retry, timeout, and circuit-breaker governance instead of adding provider-specific hidden control paths.
The adapter should carry request_id at the response or per-hit audit layer so operators can trace a paid call.

slices.title: Harden QNAIGC verification diagnostics and manual ignition guardrails
slices.exit_condition: Operator-facing diagnostics, smoke guidance, and tests make QNAIGC rate-limit behavior, fallback semantics, and paid-call activation boundaries explicit before wider routing promotion is considered.
notes: Manual ignition should start with low-volume Chinese-query smoke paths using runtime-injected QNAIGC_TOKEN_A or QNAIGC_TOKEN_B.
The cost-aware lane should fail closed on missing token, 429, or provider contract drift rather than silently becoming the default path.

slices.title: Land the repo-scoped feed-bundle and digest-delivery hardening blueprint plus manual ignition map
slices.exit_condition: The repository contains a repo-scoped blueprint that narrows the gap to replayable feed bundles, deterministic digest payloads, prompt overrides, onboarding defaults, and route-backed delivery hardening, and the active blueprint truth exposes a new manual ignition target beyond the completed L18 wave.

The resulting ignition map should make L19.2 the next narrow contract-freeze target.

slices.title: Define the feed_bundle and prepare_digest_payload contract on top of current pack feed and digest surfaces
slices.exit_condition: Repo truth explicitly defines replayable feed_bundle membership and a deterministic prepare_digest_payload boundary with content, config, prompts, stats, and errors while preserving current build_digest and emit_digest_package anchors.
notes: The contract should bind pack/profile/source scope, run window, stats, and errors without inventing a second source-catalog model.
Deterministic digest payloads must be route-ready inputs and must not re-query network data during rendering.

slices.title: Define prompt-pack override order and first-run digest profile onboarding semantics across CLI MCP and console
slices.exit_condition: Repo truth defines repo-default prompt packs, local override order, and one shared first-run digest profile for language, timezone, frequency, and default delivery target without inventing GUI-only state.
notes: Keep prompt override order explicit and operator-visible instead of hiding it in environment drift.
Onboarding must compile down to shared Reader-backed nouns rather than separate CLI and browser setup stories.

slices.title: Add Reader CLI and MCP feed-bundle export plus deterministic digest payload emission
slices.exit_condition: DataPulse can export replayable feed bundles and deterministic digest payloads that reuse current pack/feed/digest semantics while keeping rendering and delivery as downstream steps.
notes: Prefer additive methods such as feed_bundle export and prepare_digest_payload over replacing build_digest or emit_digest_package.
MCP and CLI nouns should match Reader exactly so the browser can project the same contract later.

slices.title: Harden route-backed digest and report delivery with Telegram chunk fallback and operator-visible diagnostics
slices.exit_condition: Route-backed digest and report delivery no longer rely on one silent Telegram send or a 3900-character truncation path and instead expose chunk, fallback, and attempt diagnostics as operator-visible delivery facts.
notes: Preserve attributable AlertEvent and route-health truth instead of inventing a hidden transport retry layer.
Markdown-or-plain fallback should fail closed and stay visible in delivery observations.

slices.title: Project feed-bundle browse run onboarding and delivery diagnostics into the browser console and smoke coverage
slices.exit_condition: The browser console can expose feed-bundle browse or run surfaces, digest profile onboarding, prompt-pack readiness, and route-backed delivery diagnostics without introducing a second browser-only digest model.
notes: Treat this slice as a projection of the L19 contract and runtime nouns, not as a separate frontend feature tree.
Browser smoke should cover onboarding visibility, bundle preview, and delivery diagnostic rendering at a high-signal level.

phases.title: Governance snapshot decoupling and stable runtime-evidence split
phases.goal: Separate repo truth, runtime-consumed bundle truth, and same-window governance evidence so post-CI closeout no longer reopens the repository while preserving the current manual ignition surface.

slices.title: Land the repo-scoped governance snapshot decoupling blueprint plus manual ignition map
slices.exit_condition: The repository contains an explicit blueprint for separating repo truth, runtime bundle truth, and same-window evidence truth, and the active blueprint path exposes a new manual ignition target beyond the completed L19 wave.
notes: This slice should not reopen the feed-bundle or release-window semantics already landed in L17-L19.
The next narrow target after this blueprint landing is L20.2, which freezes the concrete path-resolution and runtime-versus-evidence contract.

slices.title: Define the path-resolution and runtime-versus-evidence contract for governance snapshot decoupling
slices.exit_condition: Repo truth explicitly defines resolver-driven roots for runtime bundle, governance snapshot, and evidence bundle paths, and freezes which artifacts remain tracked runtime truth versus derived same-window evidence.
notes: Keep the operator-facing ignition commands unchanged while making the underlying roots configurable and dual-readable.
This contract should make L20.3 an implementation slice rather than another design discussion.

slices.title: Implement path resolvers and dual-read compatibility across reader loop and activation tooling
slices.exit_condition: Runtime loading, loop evaluation, and activation tooling all resolve bundle and evidence roots through the new compatibility layer while preserving the existing wrapper commands and legacy out-path fallback.
notes: The compatibility layer should prefer explicit CLI or configured roots first and retain out-path fallback during migration.
Do not make out/ha_latest_release_bundle the only admissible bundle root after this slice.

slices.title: Split stable runtime bundle truth from derived same-window evidence outputs without breaking manual ignition
verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_reader.py tests/test_report.py tests/test_delivery.py tests/test_governance_loop.py -q
slices.exit_condition: A stable tracked runtime bundle root exists for bundle-first consumption, same-window exporters write derived evidence to a separate plane, and post-CI closeout no longer depends on tracked out/* churn to preserve truth.
notes: Keep the same one-command ignition wrapper while moving high-churn evidence away from tracked main-branch truth.
This slice should preserve fail-closed attestation and replay semantics rather than weakening them.

slices.title: Retire legacy tracked out snapshot coupling and update ignore rules docs and closeout behavior
slices.exit_condition: Legacy tracked out/governance and out/*release_bundle* coupling is no longer required for terminal stop truth, ignored outputs are aligned with the new evidence plane, and the repo returns to a clean baseline after blueprint-only or closeout-only work.
notes: Do not retire legacy paths until compatibility and runtime bundle migration are already proven.
The closing proof for this wave is that manual ignition is still one command and post-CI closeout does not reopen the repo.

slices.exit_condition: The external 2026-03-30 ModelBus feedback is rewritten as DataPulse repo truth: preserve resolver-owned bundle-first roots, keep the public AI surface set at five, do not publish web_research early, and state the next ignition target explicitly.
notes: Do not import the external repo's out/ha_latest_release_bundle wording as-is; L20 already moved canonical runtime truth to resolver-owned roots with legacy out/* kept as compatibility fallback only.
The purpose of this slice is to translate external feedback into DataPulse-owned blueprint truth rather than to reopen the bridge or runtime contract from scratch.

slices.title: Expand runtime-hit and same-window attestation closure to all five public AI surfaces
slices.exit_condition: Machine-readable runtime-hit evidence and same-window attestation now require mission_suggest, triage_assist, claim_draft, and delivery_summary to verify in the same window while preserving report_draft=verified_fail_closed as required runtime closure.
notes: This slice raises repo-local ignition readiness without claiming that web_research is already a published DataPulse surface.
The attestation must stay fail-closed and should use the same bundle-first runtime root semantics that L20 froze.

slices.title: Stage internal research working-slice boundaries and formal post-fix joint-debug entry criteria
slices.exit_condition: DataPulse names research_packet, evidence_bundle, ReportBrief, and CitationBundle as repo-local working-slice objects, keeps web_research non-public until CLI/contract/runtime evidence align, and records the exact handoff criteria for the next ModelBus same-window joint-debug window.
notes: Treat ReportBrief and CitationBundle as already-landed repo objects; the new work is the repo-prep boundary, provenance wording, and post-fix joint-debug gate definition.
Do not bind provider-specific aliases or downstream provider keys directly into DataPulse business modules as part of this slice.
